# UC-14.2.12 · Historian Data Integrity

## Description

Gaps or duplicated timestamps in historian feeds break batch quality and regulatory reporting.

## Value

Gaps or duplicated timestamps in historian feeds break batch quality and regulatory reporting.

## Implementation

Ingest quality codes and source timestamps. Alert on gap > SLA or bad quality >10% of samples per tag group.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: PI / OPC-UA historian export.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="historian:point"` (value, quality, ts).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest quality codes and source timestamps. Alert on gap > SLA or bad quality >10% of samples per tag group.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="historian:point"
| sort 0 + _time
| streamstats window=2 global=prev
| eval gap_sec=_time-prev_ts
| where gap_sec > 300 OR quality!="Good"
| stats count by tag_name, gap_sec
```

Understanding this SPL

**Historian Data Integrity** — Gaps or duplicated timestamps in historian feeds break batch quality and regulatory reporting.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="historian:point"` (value, quality, ts). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): PI / OPC-UA historian export. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: historian:point. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="historian:point". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap_sec > 300 OR quality!="Good"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by tag_name, gap_sec** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (insert rate), Table (tags with gaps), Single value (data quality %).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="historian:point"
| sort 0 + _time
| streamstats window=2 global=prev
| eval gap_sec=_time-prev_ts
| where gap_sec > 300 OR quality!="Good"
| stats count by tag_name, gap_sec
```

## Visualization

Line chart (insert rate), Table (tags with gaps), Single value (data quality %).
